page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 192 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 935.0 and stress-ng single-core CPU performance, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is particularly weak, ranking in the bottom 10%. However, the instance leverages its 24 dedicated cores to achieve average multi-core performance. Multi-threaded benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core (7163.0), Passmark CPU score (18392.83), and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, consistently align with mid-range industry averages. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 984.37 GB/sec.

Given its high memory capacity and average multi-core processing capabilities, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance. Appropriate applications include database operations, caching layers like Redis (which achieved 16.8 million operations per second in testing), and software compilation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and moderate 4 Gbps network baseline positions this instance as a specialized memory resource rather than a high-throughput storage or networking node.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, it provides 192 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. However, the instance delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 7163.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.8 million operations per second. With a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing where high single-thread speed is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 4 Gbps network baseline, delivering average multi-core performance for database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring twenty-four dedicated cores and one hundred ninety-two gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY cores at 2.5 GHz
192 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per core
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for databases, caching, and multi-threaded tasks
